[2019-03-10 02:07] zera777 (Zera): @Drevoed 
"Why Cynestra is fragile (or Tesla, to a smaller extent)? I understand why Reso is, otherwise it could force draws. But how often being able to damage Cynestra by 1 decides the outcome of a game?"
Translated: "How often does 1 extra damage decide the game?"
Literally all the time. Getting breached for 1 damage is a lot less painful when you lose 1 attack instead of 3.
[2019-03-10 02:10] zera777 (Zera): I don't see my confusion.

[2019-03-10 02:11] drevoed: what would have changed if it wasn't fragile?
[2019-03-10 02:11] zera777 (Zera): Oh, I see, *that's* your question.
[2019-03-10 02:12] zera777 (Zera): Uh, it's made with green and not blue. The end.
[2019-03-10 02:13] zera777 (Zera): The difference between health and defense is more noticeable the higher the value is. At 2 HP/DEF, the difference is as small as possible without being identical.
[2019-03-10 02:17] zera777 (Zera): A Rhino can kill a unit with 2 health, but not 2 defense.

[2019-04-03 04:54] nvlx (Fate): i would probably give at least 1000:1 on chess being a draw (something i can afford without breaking me while still giving some return). the drawing margin is pretty wide, several pawn down endings are well-known holds, sometimes even 2 pawns down..
top prismata players being more confident about bso being a p2 win than about chess not being a draw seems like an effect of being relatively better at prismata than at chess. i would definitely be more confident about the chess draw but i'm not particularly good at prismata and i didn't do bso analysis by myself...
if this conclusion is reached by discarding many dumb-looking branches as implausible, i'd say that it's also pretty unlikely for everyone in chess to not have done enough research in the forced win branches. there's a lot of computational power being thrown at this in computer-assisted games as well (ICCF)..

[2019-04-03 05:07] mrguy888: If you give the upper bound, it looks like prismata might have more possiblilities, but the reality of prismata is much farther from its upper bound than chess is from its upper bound.
